calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing And Motion For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jan-06-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stuart Wilcox, Esq. 720-331-0385 Environmental Advocates for Petitioner. (Zoom)
Emily Hajarizadeh, Esq. 916-210-7795, Daniel Fuchs, Esq. 916-210-7827 Deputy Atorney General Office of the Attorney General for Respondents.
Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR #10619, 925-954-9888, raquel@transcriptlegal.com

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED. Mr. Wilcox contests and presents arguments on the three motions. After full arguments, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

3 - PETITIONER ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION's Motion For Writ Of Mandate.

Petitioner Ecological Rights Foundation's Motion for Writ of Mandate is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

The court finds that this motion is premature, as the pleadings are not yet settled.

The court orders the parties to engage in meaningful case management so that issues are presented consistent with logic, economy and fairness.

Counsel for the Respondent is required to prepare 3 separate proposed orders which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must emal to contestdept302tr@sftc.org ASAP.
(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR #10619, 925-954-9888, raquel@transcriptlegal.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Jan-08-2026 Denied

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Jul-10-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery

Petitioner Ecological Rights Foundation's motion to compel further responses to requests for admissions 1-7 and 18-30, special interrogatories 1-6 and 16, and form interrogatory 17.1 is granted in its entirety. All of the objections by respondent California State Water Resources Control Board lack merit except for attorney-client privilege and work product doctrine objections and, as to those objections, the court is unable to evaluate them without the Board providing more information supporting the objections. The Board must serve verified code-compliant further responses to all the discovery requests at issue on this motion without any objections other than attorney-client and work product objections no later than August 29, 2025. Any responsive information withheld on the grounds of attorney-client privilege and work product doctrine must be identified in a privilege log to be served concurrent with the further responses.

The Board's use of its own definition of "Triennial Review" and its objections based on non-justiciability, relevance, sovereign immunity, quasi-legislative decision-making, ripeness, official information privilege, and deliberative process privilege lack merit.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/HEK)
